evidence: In 2025, we continued our investments in Gen AI to accelerate internal productivity and improve the consumer and partner experience.
partnering with leading Gen AI organizations
Competition for well-qualified employees, especially software engineers, professionals supporting our Gen AI initiatives, and other technology professionals, is intense and costly.
We offer consumer-facing Gen AI capabilities across our brands, including trip planners, AI assistants to answer consumer queries, and a price comparison tool.
Gen AI has also improved customer service resolution times and customer satisfaction.
We offer Gen AI powered tools like Smart Messenger and Auto-Reply to enable partners to engage with guests more efficiently.

Our utilization of Gen AI may increase risks related to harmful content, inaccuracies, bias or discrimination, intellectual property infringement or misappropriation, data privacy, cybersecurity, or other issues.

Gen AI also lowers barriers to entry and enables competitors to potentially replicate or improve core functionality... AI agents may further evolve into full-service booking platforms, increasing competitive pressure and disintermediating OTCs.
These platforms increasingly incorporate Gen AI features... that may satisfy user intent without directing users to our services or may favor the platform's own or affiliated offerings.

limitations: No quantified AI-specific capex or dollar amounts disclosed in the excerpts; investment intensity scored 3 rather than 4/5 due to absence of quantified spend figures.

ai_narrative_concreteness scored 4 (deployed with operating detail) based on named deployed features and qualitative outcome claims (improved customer service resolution times, customer satisfaction); no quantified business results (%, $, volume) were provided, preventing a score of 5.
Promotional language present throughout business section; outcome claims (e.g., improved resolution times) are asserted without metrics, reducing confidence slightly.
No quantified risk treatment or named AI governance oversight bodies found in excerpts; risk disclosure scored 4 (company-specific mechanisms described) rather than 5.
